title: K2 Factor-Tilt PORTFOLIO Backtest -- Stage 0 pre-registration
decision_owner: the project (DEC-039). Harness MEASURES + RECOMMENDS; does not finalize K2 deployment.
discipline_note: Committed BEFORE any backtest is run and BEFORE any D-191 result is seen. The NET DECISION RULE (DEC-K2) is FROZEN here and will NOT be relaxed post-hoc (no threshold lowering, no horizon/window widening, no factor-set post-hoc adjustment). MaliTablo profit itemCodes are frozen via a result-independent LIVE DISCOVERY (Faz B) before this file is committed; snapshot content hashes are backfilled below before the pre-results commit. composite-optimize FORBIDDEN (invariant 4): equal-weight rank average only.
builds_on: SPEC_YOL2 v3.0 sec.1 KATMAN 2 (modest factor tilt) + sec.4 (degismez test-dersleri)
RR-OMEGA (value+quality+lowvol ~%55-65 prior; BIST-net-USD-real alpha UNTESTED -> kanit-boslugu)
D-177/D-178 (Faz 0 lowvol price factor), D-183 (Faz 0b value via MaliTablo, RR-035 rho=1.0)
D-185/D-186 (armored backtest lessons: real-DD, XU100-relative, fair-null, no nominal-drift)
hypothesis: A long-only, equal-weight, semi-annual-rebalanced tilt toward value + quality/profitability + low-vol beats an equal-weight random basket and a fair null on a NET (cost+tax+slippage) TL-real + benchmark-relative basis. Expectation REALISTIC: a few points, not multiples.
what_this_is_NOT: NOT the Faz 0 cross-sectional rank-IC test (that measured single-name selection skill and was weak). This is FACTOR-PREMIUM HARVESTING: rule-based, equal-weight, low-turnover tilt -- a different claim (confirmed by the critique-agent).

window.rebalance: semi-annual (anchors Jun-30, Dec-31; last trading day on/before)

in_out_sample.rule: in-sample = rebalance date < insample_end; out-of-sample = >=. Frozen as a DATE (not 'half').
in_out_sample.thin_n_caveat: ~6-7 periods/slice -> wide CIs / low power. CONSCIOUSLY ACCEPTED data limit (factor tilt needs fundamentals; clean pre-2018 data unavailable). Reported, NOT over-interpreted; one passing slice is not over-read.

universe.adv_floor: NONE (2019 TL volumes << 2024; a 2024-calibrated floor would wrongly trim the early window -- D-184 precedent)

survivorship.reason: delisted names not fetchable from yfinance (404)
survivorship.bias_direction: OPTIMISTIC upper-bound: excluding (mostly poor-outcome) delisted names inflates the absolute level. The FAIR NULL draws from the SAME survivors pool, so the strategy-vs-null comparison stays fair; only the absolute level is inflated. Reported in Stage-0 + results + report (the maintainer condition).

profitability.role: PRIORITY (EM-FF5: the factor that survives factor-spanning in emerging markets)
profitability.primary_ref: Novy-Marx 2013, 'The Other Side of Value: The Gross Profitability Premium', JFE 108(1):1-28
profitability.robustness: ROE = net_income / book_eaoop (equity attributable to parent)
profitability.em_ref: Hanauer & Kodongo (EM FF5); profitability spanning in EM
profitability.banks: GP/TA undefined for banks -> NULL (R3); ROE may be defined where bank net_income+book present
profitability.source: Is Yatirim MaliTablo (clean, RR-035 rho=1.0); yfinance fallback only for MaliTablo-empty names (~67% reconcile caveat applied ONLY to fallback names)

value.orientation: low P/B (cheap) -> higher rank (invert=True on P/B)

lowvol.definition: 252-day trailing realized volatility of daily log returns (factors.realized_vol)
lowvol.ref: Blitz & van Vliet 2007 (Robeco); Baker-Bradley-Wurgler low-volatility anomaly

composite.rule: equal-weight average of per-factor cross-sectional ranks (invariant 4: NO weight optimization, NO z-score, NO regression weights)
composite.eligibility: a name needs value + profitability + lowvol all present -> composite well-defined; per-date eligible-N reported (banks/par-guard reduce N)

selection_variants_N_lock.rule: N<=3 COMPOSITE selection variants; NO parameter sweep, NO new factors.
selection_variants_N_lock.single_factor_diagnostics: profitability / value / lowvol single-factor tercile portfolios are REQUIRED significance diagnostics for gate 4 -- NOT sweep variants, do NOT count against the N<=3 composite budget.

cost_tax_slippage.cost_model: round_trip_cost_pct(ticker, tier A) reused (RR-015; 2x commission + spread embedded)
cost_tax_slippage.slippage: RR-014 market impact: 20 bps/side added on turned-over notional
cost_tax_slippage.application: per rebalance: cost = one_way_turnover * (avg_round_trip + 2*slippage_oneway); one_way_turnover = 0.5*sum|w_new-w_old|. First entry charged as a round trip (slightly conservative).
cost_tax_slippage.tax: BIST equities 0% capital gains (K0). 15% dividend withholding: since yfinance auto_adjust embeds GROSS dividends in price, a frozen dividend-withholding DRAG approximates it (assumed annual yield 3% * 15% = 0.45%/yr, pro-rated). CAVEAT (assumed yield) reported.

return_basis.primary_GATE: TL-real (TUFE-deflated): real = (1+net)/(cpi1/cpi0) - 1; cpi = EVDS TP.FG.J0 monthly, daily-ffill
return_basis.reported_relative: XU100-relative: (1+net)/(1+xu_ret) - 1 (geometric excess over index; D-186 nominal-drift control)
return_basis.reported_usd: USD-real: (1+net)*(fx0/fx1) - 1 then US-CPI-deflated if a frozen US-CPI series is supplied; else USD-NOMINAL (labeled). fx = EVDS TP.DK.USD.A. No silent fallback.
return_basis.tl_real_absent_rule: if TUFE unavailable -> PRIMARY gate INCONCLUSIVE (not silently replaced by relative)

fair_null.design: portfolio-level random-selection null: at each rebalance draw the SAME N names uniformly without replacement from the SAME eligible pool; SAME rebalance dates / holding / cost+tax. Randomizes ONLY name selection.
fair_null.rationale: the edge claim is 'the factor tilt picks better names than chance' -> the null randomizes name selection while holding the rebalance/holding/cost machinery fixed (analogous to D-186 isolating entry timing).
fair_null.statistic: full-window mean per-period net TL-real return

significance.method: block-bootstrap 95% CI of the per-period net TL-real series (factor_ic_harness.block_bootstrap_ci)
significance.note: semi-annual holding periods are NON-overlapping -> near-independent -> block=1 (ordinary bootstrap)

decision_rule_DEC_K2.passes_if: ALL of: (1) PRIMARY net (cost+tax+slippage) TL-real expectation significantly POSITIVE (bootstrap 95% CI excludes zero AND CI_low > 0); (2) beats the fair portfolio null at >= 95th percentile (TL-real); (3) out-of-sample expectation also positive (in/out sign-consistent); (4) at least one factor -- especially profitability -- independently significant (its single-factor portfolio clears gates 1+2).
decision_rule_DEC_K2.fails_if: any gate fails -> modest-factor tilt weak at BIST scale -> K2 does NOT enter Yol 2; pure foundation (K0+K1) remains. (Wasted test-effort, not money lost.)
decision_rule_DEC_K2.reported_not_gates: USD-real + XU100-relative are ALWAYS reported but NEVER decide.
decision_rule_DEC_K2.no_post_hoc_relaxation: this rule is frozen; not loosened after seeing results.
decision_rule_DEC_K2.tl_real_unavailable: PRIMARY gate INCONCLUSIVE if TUFE absent.

benchmark_decision_frozen.secondary_reported: USD-real ALWAYS in the table. USD-real-positive = superior/bonus; USD-real-loss-minimized + TL-real-positive = ACCEPTABLE (SPIVA: %86-90 of active funds miss the index -> USD-real-positive alpha is hard even for the best; perfectionism would needlessly eliminate the system).

fundamentals.itemcodes_profit_discovery: EREGL XI_29, Faz B ascii-folded itemName match: 3D=BRUT KAR (gross profit, distinct from 3DF operating profit); 1BL=TOPLAM VARLIKLAR; 3Z=Ana Ortaklik Paylari (parent net income, consistent with book_eaoop=2O)

thresholds_reference.note: K2 measurement knobs live in k2_tilt_config.py (like faz0_config / trend_d186_config), NOT in thresholds.py.

config_budget: N<=3 composite selection variants (single-factor diagnostics excluded by design).
outputs: docs/k2_test/factor_tilt_results.json (after run: factor x {single,composite} x {tl_real, xu100_relative, usd_real} x fair-null x {in,out} x post-cost + VERDICT)
docs/research/D-191-k2-factor-tilt-rapor.md (DEC-K2 verdict + K2 recommendation or premise re-think)
